1	"""
2	Core data models for the Noosphere knowledge system.
3	
4	Every model is a Pydantic BaseModel for serialization, validation, and
5	JSON round-tripping to disk. The fundamental unit is the Claim — an atomic
6	proposition extracted from a transcript, attributed to a speaker, and
7	positioned in embedding space.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	from datetime import date, datetime
12	from enum import Enum
13	from typing import Optional, Any, Literal, NewType
14	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ConfigDict
15	import uuid
16	
17	
18	# ── Enums ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19	
20	class RelationType(str, Enum):
21	    """Semantic relationship between two claims or principles."""
22	    SUPPORTS = "supports"           # A provides evidence/reasoning for B
23	    CONTRADICTS = "contradicts"     # A is logically incompatible with B
24	    REFINES = "refines"             # A is a more precise version of B
25	    INSTANTIATES = "instantiates"   # A is a specific case of general B
26	    EXTENDS = "extends"             # A adds new scope to B
27	    ANALOGIZES = "analogizes"       # A draws structural parallel to B
28	    PRESUPPOSES = "presupposes"     # A requires B to be true
29	    QUALIFIES = "qualifies"         # A limits or conditions B
30	
31	
32	class Discipline(str, Enum):
33	    """Knowledge domains tracked by Theseus."""
34	    PHILOSOPHY = "Philosophy"
35	    PHYSICS = "Physics"
36	    AI = "AI"
37	    ENTREPRENEURSHIP = "Entrepreneurship"
38	    VC = "VC"
39	    ART = "Art & Film"
40	    LITERATURE = "Literature"
41	    MATHEMATICS = "Mathematics"
42	    ECONOMICS = "Economics"
43	    HISTORY = "History"
44	    EPISTEMOLOGY = "Epistemology"
45	    ETHICS = "Ethics"
46	    POLITICAL_PHILOSOPHY = "Political Philosophy"
47	    STRATEGY = "Strategy"
48	
49	
50	class ConvictionLevel(str, Enum):
51	    """How strongly a principle is held, inferred from discourse patterns."""
52	    AXIOM = "axiom"                 # Foundational, never questioned
53	    STRONG = "strong"               # Consistently asserted with emphasis
54	    MODERATE = "moderate"           # Asserted but open to refinement
55	    EXPLORATORY = "exploratory"     # Tentatively proposed, being tested
56	    CONTESTED = "contested"         # Actively debated among founders
57	
58	
59	# ── Round 3: Freshness & Decay (defined early for use in existing models) ────
60	
61	class Freshness(str, Enum):
62	    """Freshness status for revalidation tracking."""
63	    FRESH = "fresh"
64	    AGING = "aging"
65	    STALE = "stale"
66	    RETIRED = "retired"
67	
68	
69	# ── Pre-existing enums (needed by store / coherence) ────────────────────────
70	
71	class CoherenceVerdict(str, Enum):
72	    COHERE = "cohere"
73	    CONTRADICT = "contradict"
74	    UNRESOLVED = "unresolved"
75	
76	
77	class ConfidenceTier(str, Enum):
78	    FOUNDER = "founder"
79	    HIGH = "high"
80	    MODERATE = "moderate"
81	    LOW = "low"
82	    SPECULATIVE = "speculative"
83	
84	
85	class AdversarialChallengeStatus(str, Enum):
86	    PENDING = "pending"
87	    EVALUATED = "evaluated"
88	    ADDRESSED = "addressed"
89	    FATAL = "fatal"
90	    SURVIVED = "survived"
91	    FALLEN = "fallen"
92	
93	
94	class PredictiveClaimStatus(str, Enum):
95	    DRAFT = "draft"
96	    ACTIVE = "active"
97	    RESOLVED = "resolved"
98	    EXPIRED = "expired"
99	
100	
101	# ── Pre-existing data models (needed by store / coherence / adversarial) ────
102	
103	
104	class SixLayerScore(BaseModel):
105	    consistency: float = 0.0
106	    argumentation: float = 0.0
107	    probabilistic: float = 0.0
108	    geometric: float = 0.0
109	    information: float = 0.0
110	    judge: float = 0.0
111	
112	
113	class Artifact(BaseModel):
114	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
115	    uri: str = ""
116	    mime_type: str = ""
117	    byte_length: int = 0
118	    content_sha256: str = ""
119	    title: str = ""
120	    author: str = ""
121	    source_date: Optional[date] = None
122	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
123	    effective_at: Optional[datetime] = None
124	    superseded_at: Optional[datetime] = None
125	    effective_at_inferred: bool = True
126	    license_status: Optional[str] = "unknown"
127	    literature_connector: Optional[str] = ""
128	
129	
130	class Chunk(BaseModel):
131	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
132	    artifact_id: str = ""
133	    start_offset: int = 0
134	    end_offset: int = 0
135	    text: str = ""
136	    metadata: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict)
137	
138	
139	class CoherenceEvaluationPayload(BaseModel):
140	    final_verdict: CoherenceVerdict = CoherenceVerdict.UNRESOLVED
141	    aggregator_verdict: CoherenceVerdict = CoherenceVerdict.UNRESOLVED
142	    prior_scores: SixLayerScore = Field(default_factory=SixLayerScore)
143	    layer_verdicts: dict[str, str] = Field(default_factory=dict)
144	
145	
146	class DriftEvent(BaseModel):
147	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
148	    target_id: str = ""
149	    observed_at: date = Field(default_factory=date.today)
150	    drift_score: float = 0.0
151	
152	
153	class Entity(BaseModel):
154	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
155	    canonical_key: Optional[str] = None
156	    label: str = ""
157	    entity_type: str = ""
158	
159	
160	class ResearchSuggestion(BaseModel):
161	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
162	    title: str = ""
163	    summary: str = ""
164	
165	
166	class ReviewItem(BaseModel):
167	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
168	    claim_a_id: str = ""
169	    claim_b_id: str = ""
170	    reason: str = ""
171	    status: str = "open"
172	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
173	
174	
175	class AdversarialChallenge(BaseModel):
176	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
177	    conclusion_id: str = ""
178	    cluster_fingerprint: str = ""
179	    content_hash: str = ""
180	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
181	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
182	
183	
184	class VoiceProfile(BaseModel):
185	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
186	    canonical_name: str = ""
187	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
188	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
189	
190	
191	class VoicePhaseRecord(BaseModel):
192	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
193	    voice_id: str = ""
194	
195	
196	class CitationRecord(BaseModel):
197	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
198	    firm_claim_id: str = ""
199	    voice_id: str = ""
200	
201	
202	class RelativePositionMap(BaseModel):
203	    conclusion_id: str = ""
204	
205	
206	class ReadingQueueEntry(BaseModel):
207	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
208	    status: str = "queued"
209	    rationale: str = ""
210	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
211	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
212	
213	
214	class PredictiveClaim(BaseModel):
215	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
216	    author_key: Optional[str] = ""
217	    artifact_id: Optional[str] = ""
218	    status: PredictiveClaimStatus = PredictiveClaimStatus.DRAFT
219	    source_claim_id: str = ""
220	    voice_id: str = ""
221	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
222	
223	
224	class PredictionResolution(BaseModel):
225	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
226	    predictive_claim_id: str = ""
227	    resolved_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
228	
229	
230	def voice_canonical_key(name: str) -> str:
231	    return name.lower().strip().replace(" ", "_")
232	
233	
234	# ── Core Data Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
235	
236	class Speaker(BaseModel):
237	    """A participant in the podcast."""
238	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
239	    name: str
240	    role: str = "founder"  # founder | guest | moderator
241	
242	
243	class TranscriptSegment(BaseModel):
244	    """A contiguous segment of speech from one speaker."""
245	    speaker: Speaker
246	    text: str
247	    start_time: Optional[float] = None   # seconds from episode start
248	    end_time: Optional[float] = None
249	    episode_id: str = ""
250	
251	
252	class Claim(BaseModel):
253	    """
254	    An atomic proposition extracted from a transcript segment.
255	
256	    This is the fundamental unit of analysis. A claim is a single
257	    assertoric sentence that can be true or false, attributed to a
258	    speaker, and positioned in embedding space.
259	    """
260	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
261	    text: str                                   # The proposition itself
262	    speaker: Speaker                            # Who said it
263	    episode_id: str                             # Which episode
264	    episode_date: date                          # When
265	    segment_context: str = ""                   # Surrounding paragraph for disambiguation
266	    disciplines: list[Discipline] = []          # Relevant domains
267	    embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None     # SBERT embedding vector
268	    confidence: float = 1.0                     # Extraction confidence (0-1)
269	    timestamp_seconds: Optional[float] = None   # Position in episode
270	    source_id: str = ""                         # Source artifact or chunk ID
271	    voice_id: str = ""                          # Voice profile ID
272	    # Round 3 additions
273	    freshness: Freshness = Freshness.FRESH
274	    last_validated_at: Optional[datetime] = None
275	
276	
277	class Principle(BaseModel):
278	    """
279	    A distilled, stable belief held by the firm.
280	
281	    Principles are derived from clusters of claims across multiple episodes.
282	    They represent the firm's enduring intellectual commitments — the things
283	    the founders believe deeply enough to act on repeatedly.
284	    """
285	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
286	    text: str                                   # Canonical statement
287	    description: str = ""                       # Elaborated explanation
288	    disciplines: list[Discipline] = []
289	    conviction: ConvictionLevel = ConvictionLevel.MODERATE
290	    conviction_score: float = 0.5               # Continuous 0-1
291	    embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None
292	
293	    # Evidence trail
294	    supporting_claims: list[str] = []           # Claim IDs
295	    first_appeared: Optional[date] = None
296	    last_reinforced: Optional[date] = None
297	    mention_count: int = 0
298	
299	    # Coherence metrics (from the 6-layer engine)
300	    coherence_score: Optional[float] = None     # Composite Coh(Γ)
301	    consistency_score: Optional[float] = None   # S₁: formal consistency
302	    argumentation_score: Optional[float] = None # S₂: grounded extension ratio
303	    probabilistic_score: Optional[float] = None # S₃: Roche's measure
304	    geometric_score: Optional[float] = None     # S₄: embedding coherence
305	    compression_score: Optional[float] = None   # S₅: information-theoretic
306	
307	    # Metadata
308	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
309	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
310	    tags: list[str] = []

1	"""
2	Object storage abstraction: local disk (dev), MinIO (CI), S3/R2 (prod).
3	
4	Artifacts should store ``content_sha256`` + ``storage_uri``; blobs live outside the DB.
5	"""
6	
7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import os
10	from pathlib import Path
11	from typing import BinaryIO, Protocol, runtime_checkable
12	
13	
14	@runtime_checkable
15	class StorageClient(Protocol):
16	    def put_bytes(self, *, key: str, data: bytes, content_type: str = "application/octet-stream") -> str: ...
17	
18	    def open_read(self, *, key: str) -> BinaryIO: ...
19	
20	    def delete(self, *, key: str) -> None: ...
21	
22	
23	class LocalDiskStorage:
24	    """Stores under ``root / key`` (key is slash-safe, no path traversal)."""
25	
26	    def __init__(self, root: Path) -> None:
27	        self._root = root.resolve()
28	        self._root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
29	
30	    def _path(self, key: str) -> Path:
31	        safe = key.lstrip("/").replace("..", "_")
32	        p = (self._root / safe).resolve()
33	        if self._root not in p.parents and p != self._root:
34	            raise ValueError("invalid storage key")
35	        p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
36	        return p
37	
38	    def put_bytes(self, *, key: str, data: bytes, content_type: str = "") -> str:
39	        _ = content_type
40	        p = self._path(key)
41	        p.write_bytes(data)
42	        return f"file://{p}"
43	
44	    def open_read(self, *, key: str) -> BinaryIO:
45	        return open(self._path(key), "rb")
46	
47	    def delete(self, *, key: str) -> None:
48	        try:
49	            self._path(key).unlink(missing_ok=True)
50	        except OSError:
51	            pass
52	
53	
54	def storage_client_from_env() -> StorageClient:
55	    """``STORAGE_BACKEND=local|s3`` (default local). S3 uses boto3 when installed."""
56	    backend = os.environ.get("STORAGE_BACKEND", "local").lower().strip()
57	    if backend == "s3":
58	        return _S3CompatibleStorage.from_env()
59	    root = Path(os.environ.get("STORAGE_LOCAL_ROOT", "noosphere_data/blobs")).resolve()
60	    return LocalDiskStorage(root)
61	
62	
63	class _S3CompatibleStorage:
64	    """S3-compatible (AWS S3, Cloudflare R2, MinIO). Requires ``boto3``."""
65	
66	    def __init__(self, bucket: str, endpoint_url: str | None, region: str | None) -> None:
67	        try:
68	            import boto3  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
69	        except ImportError as e:
70	            raise RuntimeError("S3 backend requires boto3 (pip install boto3)") from e
71	        self._bucket = bucket
72	        kwargs: dict = {}
73	        if endpoint_url:
74	            kwargs["endpoint_url"] = endpoint_url
75	        if region:
76	            kwargs["region_name"] = region
77	        self._s3 = boto3.client("s3", **kwargs)
78	
79	    @classmethod
80	    def from_env(cls) -> StorageClient:
81	        bucket = os.environ["S3_BUCKET"]
82	        endpoint = os.environ.get("S3_ENDPOINT_URL") or None
83	        region = os.environ.get("S3_REGION") or None
84	        return cls(bucket, endpoint, region)
85	
86	    def put_bytes(self, *, key: str, data: bytes, content_type: str = "application/octet-stream") -> str:
87	        self._s3.put_object(Bucket=self._bucket, Key=key, Body=data, ContentType=content_type)
88	        return f"s3://{self._bucket}/{key}"
89	
90	    def open_read(self, *, key: str) -> BinaryIO:
91	        import io
92	
93	        obj = self._s3.get_object(Bucket=self._bucket, Key=key)
94	        return io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read())
95	
96	    def delete(self, *, key: str) -> None:
97	        self._s3.delete_object(Bucket=self._bucket, Key=key)

52	def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:

55	    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)

84	def _validate_against_schema(schema: Any, data: Any) -> Any:
85	    if isinstance(schema, type) and issubclass(schema, BaseModel):
86	        if isinstance(data, schema):
87	            return data
88	        return schema.model_validate(data)
89	    if isinstance(schema, dict) and schema:
90	        try:
91	            import jsonschema as _js
92	
93	            _js.validate(data, schema)
94	        except ImportError:
95	            pass
96	    return data

103	def _run_failure_path(
104	    spec: Method,
105	    inv: MethodInvocation,
106	    validated_input: Any,
107	    exc: BaseException,
108	) -> None:
109	    inv = inv.model_copy(
110	        update={
111	            "ended_at": _utcnow(),
112	            "succeeded": False,
113	            "error_kind": type(exc).__name__,
114	        }
115	    )
116	    for hook_name, hook in _FAILURE_HOOKS:
117	        try:
118	            hook(spec, inv, validated_input, exc)
119	        except Exception:
120	            logger.exception("Failure hook %s raised", hook_name)
121	    store = _get_store()
122	    if store is not None:
123	        try:
124	            store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
125	        except Exception:
126	            logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
127	
128	
129	def register_method(
130	    *,
131	    name: str,
132	    version: str,
133	    method_type: MethodType,
134	    input_schema: Any,
135	    output_schema: Any,
136	    description: str,
137	    rationale: str,
138	    preconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
139	    postconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
140	    dependencies: list[tuple[str, str]] | tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (),
141	    owner: str,
142	    status: str = "active",
143	    nondeterministic: bool = False,
144	    emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation] | tuple[CascadeEdgeRelation, ...] = (),
145	) -> Callable:
146	    def decorator(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
147	        if isinstance(input_schema, type) and issubclass(input_schema, BaseModel):
148	            in_schema_dict = input_schema.model_json_schema()
149	        elif isinstance(input_schema, dict):
150	            in_schema_dict = input_schema
151	        else:
152	            in_schema_dict = {}
153	
154	        if isinstance(output_schema, type) and issubclass(output_schema, BaseModel):
155	            out_schema_dict = output_schema.model_json_schema()
156	        elif isinstance(output_schema, dict):
157	            out_schema_dict = output_schema
158	        else:
159	            out_schema_dict = {}
160	
161	        impl = MethodImplRef(
162	            module=fn.__module__,
163	            fn_name=fn.__qualname__,
164	            git_sha=_current_git_sha(),
165	            image_digest=None,
166	        )
167	
168	        spec_for_hash = {
169	            "name": name,
170	            "version": version,
171	            "method_type": method_type.value,
172	            "input_schema": in_schema_dict,
173	            "output_schema": out_schema_dict,
174	            "description": description,
175	            "rationale": rationale,
176	            "preconditions": list(preconditions),
177	            "postconditions": list(postconditions),
178	            "dependencies": [list(d) for d in dependencies],
179	            "implementation": impl.model_dump(mode="json"),
180	            "owner": owner,
181	            "status": status,
182	            "nondeterministic": nondeterministic,
183	        }
184	
185	        method_id = hashlib.sha256(
186	            _canonical_json(spec_for_hash).encode()
187	        ).hexdigest()[:32]
188	
189	        spec = Method(
190	            method_id=method_id,
191	            name=name,
192	            version=version,
193	            method_type=method_type,
194	            input_schema=in_schema_dict,
195	            output_schema=out_schema_dict,
196	            description=description,
197	            rationale=rationale,
198	            preconditions=list(preconditions),
199	            postconditions=list(postconditions),
200	            dependencies=list(dependencies),
201	            implementation=impl,
202	            owner=owner,
203	            status=status,
204	            nondeterministic=nondeterministic,
205	            created_at=_utcnow(),
206	        )
207	
208	        @wraps(fn)
209	        def wrapped(input_data: Any) -> Any:
210	            validated_input = _validate_against_schema(input_schema, input_data)
211	            input_hash = hashlib.sha256(
212	                _canonical_json(validated_input).encode()
213	            ).hexdigest()
214	
215	            inv = MethodInvocation(
216	                id=str(uuid4()),
217	                method_id=spec.method_id,
218	                input_hash=input_hash,
219	                output_hash="",
220	                started_at=_utcnow(),
221	                ended_at=None,
222	                succeeded=False,
223	                error_kind=None,
224	                correlation_id=_current_correlation_id(),
225	                tenant_id=_current_tenant_id(),
226	            )
227	
228	            try:
229	                for _hook_name, hook in _PRE_HOOKS:
230	                    hook(spec, inv, validated_input)
231	            except Exception as e:
232	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
233	                raise
234	
235	            try:
236	                result = fn(validated_input)
237	                _validate_against_schema(output_schema, result)
238	                output_hash = hashlib.sha256(
239	                    _canonical_json(result).encode()
240	                ).hexdigest()
241	                inv = inv.model_copy(
242	                    update={
243	                        "ended_at": _utcnow(),
244	                        "succeeded": True,
245	                        "output_hash": output_hash,
246	                    }
247	                )
248	
249	                for hook_name, hook in _POST_HOOKS:
250	                    try:
251	                        hook(spec, inv, validated_input, result)
252	                    except Exception:
253	                        logger.exception("Post-hook %s raised", hook_name)
254	
255	                store = _get_store()
256	                if store is not None:
257	                    try:
258	                        store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
259	                    except Exception:
260	                        logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
261	
262	                return result
263	            except Exception as e:
264	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
265	                raise
266	
267	        wrapped.__method_spec__ = spec  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
268	
269	        REGISTRY.register(spec, wrapped)
270	
271	        if emits_edges:
272	            REGISTRY.set_emits_edges(method_id, list(emits_edges))
273	
274	        store = _get_store()
275	        if store is not None:
276	            try:
277	                store.insert_method(spec)
278	            except Exception:
279	                logger.exception("Failed to persist method spec")
280	
281	        return wrapped
282	
283	    return decorator

1	"""
2	Deterministic, content-addressed identifiers for domain objects.
3	"""
4	
5	from __future__ import annotations
6	
7	import hashlib
8	from pathlib import Path
9	
10	
11	def _sha256_hex(data: bytes) -> str:
12	    return hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()
13	
14	
15	def artifact_id_from_bytes(content: bytes) -> str:
16	    """Stable ID for an artifact from raw file bytes (content hash)."""
17	    return f"art_{_sha256_hex(content)}"
18	
19	
20	def artifact_id_from_file(path: str | Path) -> str:
21	    """Read file bytes and compute artifact ID (idempotent for same path+content)."""
22	    p = Path(path)
23	    return artifact_id_from_bytes(p.read_bytes())
24	
25	
26	def chunk_id(artifact_id: str, start_offset: int, end_offset: int) -> str:
27	    """Chunk ID from artifact and byte span (inclusive start, exclusive end)."""
28	    key = f"{artifact_id}:{start_offset}:{end_offset}".encode()
29	    return f"chk_{_sha256_hex(key)}"
30	
31	
32	def normalize_claim_text(text: str) -> str:
33	    """Normalize claim text before hashing (stable across trivial whitespace)."""
34	    return " ".join(text.strip().split())
35	
36	
37	def claim_text_hash(normalized_text: str) -> str:
38	    return _sha256_hex(normalized_text.encode("utf-8"))
39	
40	
41	def claim_id(chunk_id_value: str, normalized_claim_text: str) -> str:
42	    """Claim ID from parent chunk and normalized proposition text."""
43	    nh = claim_text_hash(normalized_claim_text)
44	    key = f"{chunk_id_value}:{nh}".encode()
45	    return f"clm_{_sha256_hex(key)}"

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import Any, Callable
4	
5	from noosphere.models import Method, MethodInvocation
6	
7	PreHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any], None]
8	PostHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, Any], None]
9	FailureHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, BaseException], None]
10	
11	_PRE_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, PreHook]] = []
12	_POST_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, PostHook]] = []
13	_FAILURE_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, FailureHook]] = []
14	
15	
16	def _replace_or_append(
17	    lst: list[tuple[str, Callable]], name: str, hook: Callable
18	) -> None:
19	    for i, (n, _) in enumerate(lst):
20	        if n == name:
21	            lst[i] = (name, hook)
22	            return
23	    lst.append((name, hook))
24	
25	
26	def register_pre_hook(name: str, hook: PreHook) -> None:
27	    _replace_or_append(_PRE_HOOKS, name, hook)
28	
29	
30	def register_post_hook(name: str, hook: PostHook) -> None:
31	    _replace_or_append(_POST_HOOKS, name, hook)
32	
33	
34	def register_failure_hook(name: str, hook: FailureHook) -> None:
35	    _replace_or_append(_FAILURE_HOOKS, name, hook)
36	
37	
38	def unregister_hook(name: str) -> None:
39	    for lst in (_PRE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _FAILURE_HOOKS):
40	        lst[:] = [(n, h) for n, h in lst if n != name]

    if isinstance(v, datetime):

def test_ledger_succeeds_with_correct_prev_hash() -> None:
    st = _store()
    e1 = _make_ledger_entry(prev_hash="")
    st.append_ledger_entry(e1)

    st.append_ledger_entry(e2)

def _make_ledger_entry(prev_hash: str = "", **kw) -> LedgerEntry:
    d = dict(
        entry_id=_uid(), prev_hash=prev_hash,
        timestamp=_now(),
        actor=Actor(kind="method", id="a1", display_name="Agent"),
        method_id=None,
        inputs_hash="ih", outputs_hash="oh",
        inputs_ref="ref:in", outputs_ref="ref:out",
        context=ContextMeta(tenant_id="t1", correlation_id=_uid()),
        signature="sig", signer_key_id="key1",
    )
    d.update(kw)
    return LedgerEntry(**d)


def _make_cascade_edge(inv_id: str, **kw) -> CascadeEdge:
    d = dict(
        edge_id=_uid(), src="n1", dst="n2",
        relation=CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS,
        method_invocation_id=inv_id,
        confidence=0.9, unresolved=False,

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile my findings:

## Ledger Package Prerequisites - Complete Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here are the key findings for building a ledger package:

---

### 1. **`noosphere/methods/__init__.py`**
   - **Exposes hook registration functions**: YES
   - **Signatures**:
     - `register_pre_hook(name: str, hook: PreHook) -> None`
     - `register_post_hook(name: str, hook: PostHook) -> None`
     - `register_failure_hook(name: str, hook: FailureHook) -> None`
   - **Hook type definitions** (from `_hooks.py`):
     - `PreHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any], None]`
     - `PostHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, Any], None]`
     - `FailureHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, BaseException], None]`
   - **Also exposed**: `register_method`, `set_store_factory`, `unregister_hook`, `get_method`, `REGISTRY`, `CORRELATION_ID`, `TENANT_ID`

---

### 2. **`noosphere/store.py`**
   - **Store class**: Main CRUD facade over SQLite (line 548)
   - **Ledger methods**:
     - `append_ledger_entry(entry: LedgerEntry) -> None` (line 1557)
       - Validates `prev_hash` against current ledger tail
       - Raises `LedgerChainError` if chain is broken
       - Stores entry as JSON in database
     - `ledger_tail() -> Optional[LedgerEntry]` (line 1604)
       - Returns the most recent ledger entry
       - Returns `None` if ledger is empty
   - **Related methods**:
     - `get_ledger_entry(entry_id: str) -> Optional[LedgerEntry]`
     - `iter_ledger(from_id: Optional[str] = None, to_id: Optional[str] = None) -> Iterator[LedgerEntry]`
   - **Exceptions**:
     - `LedgerChainError`: Raised when `prev_hash` doesn't match tail

---

### 3. **`noosphere/models.py`**
   - **LedgerEntry model** (lines 503-518):
     ```python
     class LedgerEntry(BaseModel):
         entry_id: str
         prev_hash: str
         timestamp: datetime
         actor: Actor
         method_id: Optional[str] = None
         inputs_hash: str
         outputs_hash: str
         inputs_ref: str
         outputs_ref: str
         context: ContextMeta
         signature: str
         signer_key_id: str
         
         model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)
     ```

   - **Actor model** (lines 485-491):
     ```python
     class Actor(BaseModel):
         kind: Literal["human", "method", "agent"]
         id: str
         display_name: str
         
         model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)
     ```

   - **ContextMeta model** (lines 494-500):
     ```python
     class ContextMeta(BaseModel):
         tenant_id: str
         correlation_id: str
         orchestrator_run_id: Optional[str] = None
         
         model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)
     ```

---

### 4. **`noosphere/storage_client.py`**
   - **Protocol definition** (lines 14-21):
     - `put_bytes(*, key: str, data: bytes, content_type: str = "application/octet-stream") -> str`
     - `open_read(*, key: str) -> BinaryIO`
     - `delete(*, key: str) -> None`
   - **Implementations**:
     - `LocalDiskStorage`: Stores files under `root / key`
     - `_S3CompatibleStorage`: S3/MinIO/R2 compatible storage (requires boto3)
   - **Factory**: `storage_client_from_env()` reads `STORAGE_BACKEND` env var (local|s3)

---

### 5. **`noosphere/methods/_decorator.py`**
   - **Method decorator**: `register_method(...)` (line 129)
   - **Key parameters**:
     - `name: str`, `version: str`, `method_type: MethodType`
     - `input_schema`, `output_schema` (Pydantic models or dicts)
     - `description`, `rationale`, `owner`
     - `preconditions`, `postconditions`, `dependencies`
     - `status` (experimental|active|deprecated|retired)
     - `nondeterministic`, `emits_edges`
   - **Hook interaction** (lines 228-265):
     - **Pre-hooks** called before function execution (line 229)
     - **Post-hooks** called after successful execution (line 249)
     - **Failure-hooks** called on exception (line 103)
   - **Store integration** (lines 255-260, 274-279):
     - Optionally persists `MethodInvocation` records via `store.insert_method_invocation()`
     - Persists method specs via `store.insert_method()`
   - **Hash computation**: Canonical JSON hashing for inputs/outputs
   - **Metadata**: `CORRELATION_ID`, `TENANT_ID` context vars automatically captured

---

### 6. **Dependency Check**
   - **`noosphere/requirements.txt`** found (26 dependencies listed)
   - **pynacl**: NOT currently listed
   - **Available cryptography libs**:
     - No PyNaCl dependency
     - No cryptography library explicitly listed
   - **Note**: Will need to add `pynacl` if building a signing/verification ledger package

---

### 7. **`noosphere/ids.py`**
   - **ID generation utilities** (lines 11-46):
     - `artifact_id_from_bytes(content: bytes) -> str` — Returns `art_{sha256_hex}`
     - `artifact_id_from_file(path: str | Path) -> str` — Content-addressed ID
     - `chunk_id(artifact_id: str, start_offset: int, end_offset: int) -> str` — Returns `chk_{sha256_hex}`
     - `normalize_claim_text(text: str) -> str` — Whitespace normalization
     - `claim_text_hash(normalized_text: str) -> str` — SHA256 hash
     - `claim_id(chunk_id_value: str, normalized_claim_text: str) -> str` — Returns `clm_{sha256_hex}`
   - **Pattern**: All IDs use SHA256 hashing with deterministic, idempotent generation

---

### Key Design Patterns
1. **Immutable models**: All three ledger-related models use `frozen=True` in `ConfigDict`
2. **Chain validation**: `append_ledger_entry` validates `prev_hash` for integrity
3. **Context tracking**: `CORRELATION_ID` and `TENANT_ID` context vars for distributed tracing
4. **Actor abstraction**: Ledger entries attribute changes to human/method/agent actors
5. **Dual hashing**: Both inputs and outputs hashed for audit trail
6. **Storage references**: `inputs_ref` and `outputs_ref` point to external blobs (via StorageClient)
7. **Deterministic IDs**: Content-addressed identifiers enable caching and deduplication

file.content:
from noosphere.methods._decorator import (
    CORRELATION_ID,
    TENANT_ID,
    register_method,
    set_store_factory,
)
from noosphere.methods._hooks import (
    register_failure_hook,
    register_post_hook,
    register_pre_hook,
    unregister_hook,
)
from noosphere.methods._registry import (
    REGISTRY,
    MethodCollisionError,
    MethodNotFoundError,
)


def get_method(
    name: str, version: str = "latest", include_retired: bool = False
):
    return REGISTRY.get(name, version=version, include_retired=include_retired)


__all__ = [
    "CORRELATION_ID",
    "REGISTRY",
    "TENANT_ID",
    "MethodCollisionError",
    "MethodNotFoundError",
    "get_method",
    "register_failure_hook",
    "register_method",
    "register_post_hook",
    "register_pre_hook",
    "set_store_factory",
    "unregister_hook",
]

23	from noosphere.methods._hooks import _FAILURE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _PRE_HOOKS
24	from noosphere.methods._registry import REGISTRY
25	
26	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
27	
28	CORRELATION_ID: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar(
29	    "CORRELATION_ID", default=""
30	)
31	TENANT_ID: contextvars.ContextVar[str] = contextvars.ContextVar(
32	    "TENANT_ID", default="default"
33	)

35	_store_factory: Callable[..., Any] | None = None
36	
37	
38	def set_store_factory(factory: Callable[..., Any]) -> None:
39	    global _store_factory
40	    _store_factory = factory
41	
42	
43	def _get_store() -> Any:
44	    if _store_factory is not None:
45	        try:
46	            return _store_factory()
47	        except Exception:
48	            return None
49	    return None
50	
51	
52	def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
53	    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
54	        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
55	    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)
56	
57	
58	def _current_git_sha() -> str:
59	    sha = os.environ.get("THESEUS_GIT_SHA")
60	    if sha:
61	        return sha
62	    try:
63	        result = subprocess.run(
64	            ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
65	            capture_output=True,
66	            text=True,
67	            timeout=5,
68	        )
69	        if result.returncode == 0:
70	            return result.stdout.strip()
71	    except Exception:
72	        pass
73	    return "unknown"
74	
75	
76	def _current_correlation_id() -> str:
77	    return CORRELATION_ID.get()
78	
79	
80	def _current_tenant_id() -> str:
81	    return TENANT_ID.get()
82	
83	
84	def _validate_against_schema(schema: Any, data: Any) -> Any:
85	    if isinstance(schema, type) and issubclass(schema, BaseModel):
86	        if isinstance(data, schema):
87	            return data
88	        return schema.model_validate(data)
89	    if isinstance(schema, dict) and schema:
90	        try:
91	            import jsonschema as _js
92	
93	            _js.validate(data, schema)
94	        except ImportError:
95	            pass
96	    return data
97	
98	
99	def _utcnow() -> datetime:
100	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
101	
102	
103	def _run_failure_path(
104	    spec: Method,
105	    inv: MethodInvocation,
106	    validated_input: Any,
107	    exc: BaseException,
108	) -> None:
109	    inv = inv.model_copy(
110	        update={
111	            "ended_at": _utcnow(),
112	            "succeeded": False,
113	            "error_kind": type(exc).__name__,
114	        }
115	    )
116	    for hook_name, hook in _FAILURE_HOOKS:
117	        try:
118	            hook(spec, inv, validated_input, exc)
119	        except Exception:
120	            logger.exception("Failure hook %s raised", hook_name)
121	    store = _get_store()
122	    if store is not None:
123	        try:
124	            store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
125	        except Exception:
126	            logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
127	
128	
129	def register_method(
130	    *,
131	    name: str,
132	    version: str,
133	    method_type: MethodType,
134	    input_schema: Any,
135	    output_schema: Any,
136	    description: str,
137	    rationale: str,
138	    preconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
139	    postconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
140	    dependencies: list[tuple[str, str]] | tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (),
141	    owner: str,
142	    status: str = "active",
143	    nondeterministic: bool = False,
144	    emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation] | tuple[CascadeEdgeRelation, ...] = (),
145	) -> Callable:
146	    def decorator(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
147	        if isinstance(input_schema, type) and issubclass(input_schema, BaseModel):
148	            in_schema_dict = input_schema.model_json_schema()
149	        elif isinstance(input_schema, dict):
150	            in_schema_dict = input_schema
151	        else:
152	            in_schema_dict = {}
153	
154	        if isinstance(output_schema, type) and issubclass(output_schema, BaseModel):
155	            out_schema_dict = output_schema.model_json_schema()
156	        elif isinstance(output_schema, dict):
157	            out_schema_dict = output_schema
158	        else:
159	            out_schema_dict = {}
160	
161	        impl = MethodImplRef(
162	            module=fn.__module__,
163	            fn_name=fn.__qualname__,
164	            git_sha=_current_git_sha(),
165	            image_digest=None,
166	        )
167	
168	        spec_for_hash = {
169	            "name": name,
170	            "version": version,
171	            "method_type": method_type.value,
172	            "input_schema": in_schema_dict,
173	            "output_schema": out_schema_dict,
174	            "description": description,
175	            "rationale": rationale,
176	            "preconditions": list(preconditions),
177	            "postconditions": list(postconditions),
178	            "dependencies": [list(d) for d in dependencies],
179	            "implementation": impl.model_dump(mode="json"),
180	            "owner": owner,
181	            "status": status,
182	            "nondeterministic": nondeterministic,
183	        }
184	
185	        method_id = hashlib.sha256(
186	            _canonical_json(spec_for_hash).encode()
187	        ).hexdigest()[:32]
188	
189	        spec = Method(
190	            method_id=method_id,
191	            name=name,
192	            version=version,
193	            method_type=method_type,
194	            input_schema=in_schema_dict,
195	            output_schema=out_schema_dict,
196	            description=description,
197	            rationale=rationale,
198	            preconditions=list(preconditions),
199	            postconditions=list(postconditions),
200	            dependencies=list(dependencies),
201	            implementation=impl,
202	            owner=owner,
203	            status=status,
204	            nondeterministic=nondeterministic,
205	            created_at=_utcnow(),
206	        )
207	
208	        @wraps(fn)
209	        def wrapped(input_data: Any) -> Any:
210	            validated_input = _validate_against_schema(input_schema, input_data)
211	            input_hash = hashlib.sha256(
212	                _canonical_json(validated_input).encode()
213	            ).hexdigest()
214	
215	            inv = MethodInvocation(
216	                id=str(uuid4()),
217	                method_id=spec.method_id,
218	                input_hash=input_hash,
219	                output_hash="",
220	                started_at=_utcnow(),
221	                ended_at=None,
222	                succeeded=False,
223	                error_kind=None,
224	                correlation_id=_current_correlation_id(),
225	                tenant_id=_current_tenant_id(),
226	            )
227	
228	            try:
229	                for _hook_name, hook in _PRE_HOOKS:
230	                    hook(spec, inv, validated_input)
231	            except Exception as e:
232	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
233	                raise
234	
235	            try:
236	                result = fn(validated_input)
237	                _validate_against_schema(output_schema, result)
238	                output_hash = hashlib.sha256(
239	                    _canonical_json(result).encode()
240	                ).hexdigest()
241	                inv = inv.model_copy(
242	                    update={
243	                        "ended_at": _utcnow(),
244	                        "succeeded": True,
245	                        "output_hash": output_hash,
246	                    }
247	                )
248	
249	                for hook_name, hook in _POST_HOOKS:
250	                    try:
251	                        hook(spec, inv, validated_input, result)
252	                    except Exception:
253	                        logger.exception("Post-hook %s raised", hook_name)
254	
255	                store = _get_store()
256	                if store is not None:
257	                    try:
258	                        store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
259	                    except Exception:
260	                        logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
261	
262	                return result
263	            except Exception as e:
264	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
265	                raise
266	
267	        wrapped.__method_spec__ = spec  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
268	
269	        REGISTRY.register(spec, wrapped)
270	
271	        if emits_edges:
272	            REGISTRY.set_emits_edges(method_id, list(emits_edges))
273	
274	        store = _get_store()
275	        if store is not None:
276	            try:
277	                store.insert_method(spec)
278	            except Exception:
279	                logger.exception("Failed to persist method spec")
280	
281	        return wrapped
282	
283	    return decorator

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import Any, Callable
4	
5	from noosphere.models import Method, MethodInvocation
6	
7	PreHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any], None]
8	PostHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, Any], None]
9	FailureHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, BaseException], None]
10	
11	_PRE_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, PreHook]] = []
12	_POST_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, PostHook]] = []
13	_FAILURE_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, FailureHook]] = []
14	
15	
16	def _replace_or_append(
17	    lst: list[tuple[str, Callable]], name: str, hook: Callable
18	) -> None:
19	    for i, (n, _) in enumerate(lst):
20	        if n == name:
21	            lst[i] = (name, hook)
22	            return
23	    lst.append((name, hook))
24	
25	
26	def register_pre_hook(name: str, hook: PreHook) -> None:
27	    _replace_or_append(_PRE_HOOKS, name, hook)
28	
29	
30	def register_post_hook(name: str, hook: PostHook) -> None:
31	    _replace_or_append(_POST_HOOKS, name, hook)
32	
33	
34	def register_failure_hook(name: str, hook: FailureHook) -> None:
35	    _replace_or_append(_FAILURE_HOOKS, name, hook)
36	
37	
38	def unregister_hook(name: str) -> None:
39	    for lst in (_PRE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _FAILURE_HOOKS):
40	        lst[:] = [(n, h) for n, h in lst if n != name]
41

2	Object storage abstraction: local disk (dev), MinIO (CI), S3/R2 (prod).
3	
4	Artifacts should store ``content_sha256`` + ``storage_uri``; blobs live outside the DB.

7	from __future__ import annotations
8	
9	import os
10	from pathlib import Path
11	from typing import BinaryIO, Protocol, runtime_checkable
12	
13	
14	@runtime_checkable
15	class StorageClient(Protocol):
16	    def put_bytes(self, *, key: str, data: bytes, content_type: str = "application/octet-stream") -> str: ...
17	
18	    def open_read(self, *, key: str) -> BinaryIO: ...
19	
20	    def delete(self, *, key: str) -> None: ...
21

23	class LocalDiskStorage:
24	    """Stores under ``root / key`` (key is slash-safe, no path traversal)."""
25	
26	    def __init__(self, root: Path) -> None:
27	        self._root = root.resolve()
28	        self._root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
29	
30	    def _path(self, key: str) -> Path:
31	        safe = key.lstrip("/").replace("..", "_")
32	        p = (self._root / safe).resolve()
33	        if self._root not in p.parents and p != self._root:
34	            raise ValueError("invalid storage key")
35	        p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
36	        return p
37	
38	    def put_bytes(self, *, key: str, data: bytes, content_type: str = "") -> str:
39	        _ = content_type
40	        p = self._path(key)
41	        p.write_bytes(data)
42	        return f"file://{p}"
43	
44	    def open_read(self, *, key: str) -> BinaryIO:
45	        return open(self._path(key), "rb")
46	
47	    def delete(self, *, key: str) -> None:
48	        try:
49	            self._path(key).unlink(missing_ok=True)
50	        except OSError:
51	            pass
52	
53	
54	def storage_client_from_env() -> StorageClient:
55	    """``STORAGE_BACKEND=local|s3`` (default local). S3 uses boto3 when installed."""
56	    backend = os.environ.get("STORAGE_BACKEND", "local").lower().strip()
57	    if backend == "s3":
58	        return _S3CompatibleStorage.from_env()
59	    root = Path(os.environ.get("STORAGE_LOCAL_ROOT", "noosphere_data/blobs")).resolve()
60	    return LocalDiskStorage(root)
61	
62	
63	class _S3CompatibleStorage:
64	    """S3-compatible (AWS S3, Cloudflare R2, MinIO). Requires ``boto3``."""
65	
66	    def __init__(self, bucket: str, endpoint_url: str | None, region: str | None) -> None:
67	        try:
68	            import boto3  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
69	        except ImportError as e:
70	            raise RuntimeError("S3 backend requires boto3 (pip install boto3)") from e
71	        self._bucket = bucket
72	        kwargs: dict = {}
73	        if endpoint_url:
74	            kwargs["endpoint_url"] = endpoint_url
75	        if region:
76	            kwargs["region_name"] = region
77	        self._s3 = boto3.client("s3", **kwargs)
78	
79	    @classmethod
80	    def from_env(cls) -> StorageClient:
81	        bucket = os.environ["S3_BUCKET"]
82	        endpoint = os.environ.get("S3_ENDPOINT_URL") or None
83	        region = os.environ.get("S3_REGION") or None
84	        return cls(bucket, endpoint, region)
85	
86	    def put_bytes(self, *, key: str, data: bytes, content_type: str = "application/octet-stream") -> str:
87	        self._s3.put_object(Bucket=self._bucket, Key=key, Body=data, ContentType=content_type)
88	        return f"s3://{self._bucket}/{key}"
89	
90	    def open_read(self, *, key: str) -> BinaryIO:
91	        import io
92	
93	        obj = self._s3.get_object(Bucket=self._bucket, Key=key)
94	        return io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read())
95	
96	    def delete(self, *, key: str) -> None:
97	        self._s3.delete_object(Bucket=self._bucket, Key=key)

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import logging
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Optional

from pydantic import BaseModel

from noosphere.models import Actor, ContextMeta, LedgerEntry
from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.keys import KeyRing

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)


def _now_isoformat() -> str:
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()


class Ledger:
    """Append-only signed, Merkle-chained audit log."""

    def __init__(self, store: Any, keyring: Optional[KeyRing] = None) -> None:
        self._store = store
        self._keyring = keyring

    @property
    def store(self) -> Any:
        return self._store

    @property
    def keyring(self) -> KeyRing:
        if self._keyring is None:
            raise RuntimeError("KeyRing not configured")
        return self._keyring

    def append(
        self,
        *,
        actor: Actor,
        method_id: str | None,
        inputs_hash: str,
        outputs_hash: str,
        inputs_ref: str,
        outputs_ref: str,
        context: ContextMeta,
    ) -> str:
        prev = self._store.ledger_tail()
        prev_hash = prev.entry_id if prev else ""
        payload = _canonical_json({
            "prev_hash": prev_hash,
            "timestamp": _now_isoformat(),
            "actor": actor.model_dump(mode="json"),
            "method_id": method_id,
            "inputs_hash": inputs_hash,
            "outputs_hash": outputs_hash,
            "inputs_ref": inputs_ref,
            "outputs_ref": outputs_ref,
            "context": context.model_dump(mode="json"),
        })
        entry_id = hashlib.sha256(
            prev_hash.encode() + payload.encode()
        ).hexdigest()
        signature_bytes = self.keyring.sign(entry_id.encode())
        entry = LedgerEntry(
            entry_id=entry_id,
            prev_hash=prev_hash,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            actor=actor,
            method_id=method_id,
            inputs_hash=inputs_hash,
            outputs_hash=outputs_hash,
            inputs_ref=inputs_ref,
            outputs_ref=outputs_ref,
            context=context,
            signature=signature_bytes.hex(),
            signer_key_id=self.keyring.active_key_id,
        )
        self._store.append_ledger_entry(entry)
        self._mirror_to_jsonl(entry)
        return entry_id

    def _mirror_to_jsonl(self, entry: LedgerEntry) -> None:
        mirror_dir = Path(
            os.environ.get("THESEUS_LEDGER_MIRROR_DIR", "~/.theseus/ledger")
        ).expanduser()
        try:
            mirror_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            filename = entry.timestamp.strftime("%Y-%m") + ".jsonl"
            filepath = mirror_dir / filename
            with open(filepath, "a") as f:
                f.write(entry.model_dump_json() + "\n")
        except Exception:
            logger.warning("Failed to mirror ledger entry to JSONL", exc_info=True)

        payload = _canonical_json({
            "prev_hash": prev_hash,
            "timestamp": _now_isoformat(),
            "actor": actor.model_dump(mode="json"),
            "method_id": method_id,
            "inputs_hash": inputs_hash,
            "outputs_hash": outputs_hash,
            "inputs_ref": inputs_ref,
            "outputs_ref": outputs_ref,
            "context": context.model_dump(mode="json"),
        })
        entry_id = hashlib.sha256(
            prev_hash.encode() + payload.encode()
        ).hexdigest()
        signature_bytes = self.keyring.sign(entry_id.encode())
        entry = LedgerEntry(
            entry_id=entry_id,
            prev_hash=prev_hash,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            actor=actor,
            method_id=method_id,
            inputs_hash=inputs_hash,
            outputs_hash=outputs_hash,
            inputs_ref=inputs_ref,
            outputs_ref=outputs_ref,
            context=context,
            signature=signature_bytes.hex(),
            signer_key_id=self.keyring.active_key_id,
        )
        self._store.append_ledger_entry(entry)
        self._mirror_to_jsonl(entry)
        return entry_id

    def _mirror_to_jsonl(self, entry: LedgerEntry) -> None:
        mirror_dir = Path(
            os.environ.get("THESEUS_LEDGER_MIRROR_DIR", "~/.theseus/ledger")
        ).expanduser()
        try:
            mirror_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            filename = entry.timestamp.strftime("%Y-%m") + ".jsonl"
            filepath = mirror_dir / filename
            with open(filepath, "a") as f:
                f.write(entry.model_dump_json() + "\n")
        except Exception:
            logger.warning("Failed to mirror ledger entry to JSONL", exc_info=True)

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import logging
from typing import Any

from pydantic import BaseModel

from noosphere.models import Actor, ContextMeta, Method, MethodInvocation

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_seen_invocations: set[str] = set()

_invocation_refs: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {}


def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)


def _pre_capture_inputs(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any) -> None:
    if inv.id in _seen_invocations:
        return
    from noosphere.storage_client import storage_client_from_env

    storage = storage_client_from_env()
    serialized = _canonical_json(input_data).encode()
    key = f"ledger/inputs/{hashlib.sha256(serialized).hexdigest()}"
    blob_ref = storage.put_bytes(key=key, data=serialized, content_type="application/json")
    _invocation_refs[inv.id] = {"inputs_ref": blob_ref}


def _post_append(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any, result: Any) -> None:
    if inv.id in _seen_invocations:
        return
    _seen_invocations.add(inv.id)

    from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.ledger import Ledger
    from noosphere.methods._decorator import _get_store
    from noosphere.storage_client import storage_client_from_env

    store = _get_store()
    if store is None:
        logger.warning("No store available for ledger append")
        return

    storage = storage_client_from_env()
    serialized = _canonical_json(result).encode()
    key = f"ledger/outputs/{hashlib.sha256(serialized).hexdigest()}"
    outputs_blob_ref = storage.put_bytes(key=key, data=serialized, content_type="application/json")

    refs = _invocation_refs.pop(inv.id, {})
    inputs_ref = refs.get("inputs_ref", "")

    from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.keys import KeyRing

    try:
        keyring = KeyRing()
    except Exception:
        logger.warning("Ledger keyring not configured, skipping append")
        return

    ledger = Ledger(store, keyring)
    ledger.append(
        actor=Actor(kind="method", id=spec.method_id, display_name=spec.name),
        method_id=spec.method_id,
        inputs_hash=inv.input_hash,
        outputs_hash=inv.output_hash,
        inputs_ref=inputs_ref,
        outputs_ref=outputs_blob_ref,
        context=ContextMeta(
            tenant_id=inv.tenant_id,
            correlation_id=inv.correlation_id,
        ),
    )


def _on_failure_append(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any, exc: BaseException) -> None:
    if inv.id in _seen_invocations:
        return
    _seen_invocations.add(inv.id)

    from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.ledger import Ledger
    from noosphere.methods._decorator import _get_store

    store = _get_store()
    if store is None:
        logger.warning("No store available for ledger failure append")
        return

    refs = _invocation_refs.pop(inv.id, {})
    inputs_ref = refs.get("inputs_ref", "")

    from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.keys import KeyRing

    try:
        keyring = KeyRing()
    except Exception:
        logger.warning("Ledger keyring not configured, skipping failure append")
        return

    ledger = Ledger(store, keyring)
    ledger.append(
        actor=Actor(kind="method", id=spec.method_id, display_name=spec.name),
        method_id=spec.method_id,
        inputs_hash=inv.input_hash,
        outputs_hash="",
        inputs_ref=inputs_ref,
        outputs_ref="",
        context=ContextMeta(
            tenant_id=inv.tenant_id,
            correlation_id=inv.correlation_id,
        ),
    )


def register_ledger_hooks() -> None:
    from noosphere.methods import register_pre_hook, register_post_hook, register_failure_hook

    register_pre_hook("ledger.capture_inputs", _pre_capture_inputs)
    register_post_hook("ledger.append", _post_append)
    register_failure_hook("ledger.append_on_failure", _on_failure_append)

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Any, Optional

from noosphere.models import LedgerEntry
from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.keys import KeyRing

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@dataclass
class EntryIssue:
    entry_id: str
    issue_type: str
    detail: str


@dataclass
class ReplayResult:
    entry_id: str
    method_id: str
    expected_output_hash: str
    actual_output_hash: str
    matched: bool
    skip_reason: str = ""


@dataclass
class VerifyReport:
    total_entries: int = 0
    chain_valid: bool = True
    signatures_valid: bool = True
    issues: list[EntryIssue] = field(default_factory=list)
    replay_results: list[ReplayResult] = field(default_factory=list)

    @property
    def ok(self) -> bool:
        return self.chain_valid and self.signatures_valid and len(self.issues) == 0


def verify(
    store: Any,
    keyring: KeyRing,
    *,
    since: Optional[str] = None,
    until: Optional[str] = None,
    replay: bool = False,
    storage_client: Any = None,
) -> VerifyReport:
    report = VerifyReport()
    prev_hash = ""
    prev_entry_id: Optional[str] = None

    if since is not None:
        before = store.get_ledger_entry(since)
        if before is not None:
            prev_hash = before.prev_hash

    entries: list[LedgerEntry] = list(store.iter_ledger(from_id=since, to_id=until))
    report.total_entries = len(entries)

    for i, entry in enumerate(entries):
        expected_prev = prev_hash if i == 0 and since is None else (
            entries[i - 1].entry_id if i > 0 else prev_hash
        )
        if entry.prev_hash != expected_prev:
            report.chain_valid = False
            report.issues.append(EntryIssue(
                entry_id=entry.entry_id,
                issue_type="chain_break",
                detail=f"prev_hash {entry.prev_hash!r} != expected {expected_prev!r}",
            ))

        sig_ok = keyring.verify(
            entry.entry_id.encode(),
            bytes.fromhex(entry.signature),
            entry.signer_key_id,
        )
        if not sig_ok:
            report.signatures_valid = False
            report.issues.append(EntryIssue(
                entry_id=entry.entry_id,
                issue_type="bad_signature",
                detail=f"Signature verification failed for key_id {entry.signer_key_id!r}",
            ))

        if replay and entry.method_id and storage_client is not None:
            _try_replay(entry, report, storage_client)

    return report


def _try_replay(
    entry: LedgerEntry,
    report: VerifyReport,
    storage_client: Any,
) -> None:
    from noosphere.methods._registry import REGISTRY

    try:
        spec, fn = _find_method_by_id(REGISTRY, entry.method_id)
    except Exception:
        report.replay_results.append(ReplayResult(
            entry_id=entry.entry_id,
            method_id=entry.method_id or "",
            expected_output_hash=entry.outputs_hash,
            actual_output_hash="",
            matched=False,
            skip_reason="method_not_found",
        ))
        return

    if spec.nondeterministic:
        report.replay_results.append(ReplayResult(
            entry_id=entry.entry_id,
            method_id=entry.method_id or "",
            expected_output_hash=entry.outputs_hash,
            actual_output_hash="",
            matched=False,
            skip_reason="nondeterministic",
        ))
        return

    try:
        import json
        raw = storage_client.open_read(key=_ref_to_key(entry.inputs_ref)).read()
        input_data = json.loads(raw)
        result = fn(input_data)
        import json as _json
        from pydantic import BaseModel
        if isinstance(result, BaseModel):
            result = result.model_dump(mode="json")
        actual_hash = hashlib.sha256(
            _json.dumps(result, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str).encode()
        ).hexdigest()
        report.replay_results.append(ReplayResult(
            entry_id=entry.entry_id,
            method_id=entry.method_id or "",
            expected_output_hash=entry.outputs_hash,
            actual_output_hash=actual_hash,
            matched=(actual_hash == entry.outputs_hash),
        ))
        if actual_hash != entry.outputs_hash:
            report.issues.append(EntryIssue(
                entry_id=entry.entry_id,
                issue_type="replay_mismatch",
                detail=f"expected {entry.outputs_hash}, got {actual_hash}",
            ))
    except Exception as e:
        report.replay_results.append(ReplayResult(
            entry_id=entry.entry_id,
            method_id=entry.method_id or "",
            expected_output_hash=entry.outputs_hash,
            actual_output_hash="",
            matched=False,
            skip_reason=f"replay_error: {e}",
        ))


def _find_method_by_id(registry: Any, method_id: str) -> tuple:
    for spec in registry.list():
        if spec.method_id == method_id:
            return registry.get(spec.name, version=spec.version)
    raise LookupError(f"Method {method_id} not found in registry")


def _ref_to_key(ref: str) -> str:
    if ref.startswith("file://"):
        return ref[7:].split("/blobs/", 1)[-1] if "/blobs/" in ref else ref[7:]
    return ref

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import logging
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Optional

from pydantic import BaseModel

from noosphere.models import Actor, ContextMeta, LedgerEntry
from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.keys import KeyRing

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)


def _now_isoformat() -> str:
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()


class Ledger:
    """Append-only signed, Merkle-chained audit log."""

    def __init__(self, store: Any, keyring: Optional[KeyRing] = None) -> None:
        self._store = store
        self._keyring = keyring

    @property
    def store(self) -> Any:
        return self._store

    @property
    def keyring(self) -> KeyRing:
        if self._keyring is None:
            raise RuntimeError("KeyRing not configured")
        return self._keyring

    def append(
        self,
        *,
        actor: Actor,
        method_id: str | None,
        inputs_hash: str,
        outputs_hash: str,
        inputs_ref: str,
        outputs_ref: str,
        context: ContextMeta,
    ) -> str:
        prev = self._store.ledger_tail()
        prev_hash = prev.entry_id if prev else ""
        payload = _canonical_json({
            "prev_hash": prev_hash,
            "timestamp": _now_isoformat(),
            "actor": actor.model_dump(mode="json"),
            "method_id": method_id,
            "inputs_hash": inputs_hash,
            "outputs_hash": outputs_hash,
            "inputs_ref": inputs_ref,
            "outputs_ref": outputs_ref,
            "context": context.model_dump(mode="json"),
        })
        entry_id = hashlib.sha256(
            prev_hash.encode() + payload.encode()
        ).hexdigest()
        signature_bytes = self.keyring.sign(entry_id.encode())
        entry = LedgerEntry(
            entry_id=entry_id,
            prev_hash=prev_hash,
            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
            actor=actor,
            method_id=method_id,
            inputs_hash=inputs_hash,
            outputs_hash=outputs_hash,
            inputs_ref=inputs_ref,
            outputs_ref=outputs_ref,
            context=context,
            signature=signature_bytes.hex(),
            signer_key_id=self.keyring.active_key_id,
        )
        self._store.append_ledger_entry(entry)
        self._mirror_to_jsonl(entry)
        return entry_id

    def _mirror_to_jsonl(self, entry: LedgerEntry) -> None:
        mirror_dir = Path(
            os.environ.get("THESEUS_LEDGER_MIRROR_DIR", "~/.theseus/ledger")
        ).expanduser()
        try:
            mirror_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            filename = entry.timestamp.strftime("%Y-%m") + ".jsonl"
            filepath = mirror_dir / filename
            with open(filepath, "a") as f:
                f.write(entry.model_dump_json() + "\n")
        except Exception:
            logger.warning("Failed to mirror ledger entry to JSONL", exc_info=True)

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import logging
from typing import Any

from pydantic import BaseModel

from noosphere.models import Actor, ContextMeta, Method, MethodInvocation

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_seen_invocations: set[str] = set()

_invocation_refs: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {}


def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)


def _pre_capture_inputs(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any) -> None:
    if inv.id in _seen_invocations:
        return
    from noosphere.storage_client import storage_client_from_env

    storage = storage_client_from_env()
    serialized = _canonical_json(input_data).encode()
    key = f"ledger/inputs/{hashlib.sha256(serialized).hexdigest()}"
    blob_ref = storage.put_bytes(key=key, data=serialized, content_type="application/json")
    _invocation_refs[inv.id] = {"inputs_ref": blob_ref}


def _post_append(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any, result: Any) -> None:
    if inv.id in _seen_invocations:
        return
    _seen_invocations.add(inv.id)

    from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.ledger import Ledger
    from noosphere.methods._decorator import _get_store
    from noosphere.storage_client import storage_client_from_env

    store = _get_store()
    if store is None:
        logger.warning("No store available for ledger append")
        return

    storage = storage_client_from_env()
    serialized = _canonical_json(result).encode()
    key = f"ledger/outputs/{hashlib.sha256(serialized).hexdigest()}"
    outputs_blob_ref = storage.put_bytes(key=key, data=serialized, content_type="application/json")

    refs = _invocation_refs.pop(inv.id, {})
    inputs_ref = refs.get("inputs_ref", "")

    from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.keys import KeyRing

    try:
        keyring = KeyRing()
    except Exception:
        logger.warning("Ledger keyring not configured, skipping append")
        return

    ledger = Ledger(store, keyring)
    ledger.append(
        actor=Actor(kind="method", id=spec.method_id, display_name=spec.name),
        method_id=spec.method_id,
        inputs_hash=inv.input_hash,
        outputs_hash=inv.output_hash,
        inputs_ref=inputs_ref,
        outputs_ref=outputs_blob_ref,
        context=ContextMeta(
            tenant_id=inv.tenant_id,
            correlation_id=inv.correlation_id,
        ),
    )


def _on_failure_append(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any, exc: BaseException) -> None:
    if inv.id in _seen_invocations:
        return
    _seen_invocations.add(inv.id)

    from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.ledger import Ledger
    from noosphere.methods._decorator import _get_store

    store = _get_store()
    if store is None:
        logger.warning("No store available for ledger failure append")
        return

    refs = _invocation_refs.pop(inv.id, {})
    inputs_ref = refs.get("inputs_ref", "")

    from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.keys import KeyRing

    try:
        keyring = KeyRing()
    except Exception:
        logger.warning("Ledger keyring not configured, skipping failure append")
        return

    ledger = Ledger(store, keyring)
    ledger.append(
        actor=Actor(kind="method", id=spec.method_id, display_name=spec.name),
        method_id=spec.method_id,
        inputs_hash=inv.input_hash,
        outputs_hash="",
        inputs_ref=inputs_ref,
        outputs_ref="",
        context=ContextMeta(
            tenant_id=inv.tenant_id,
            correlation_id=inv.correlation_id,
        ),
    )


def register_ledger_hooks() -> None:
    from noosphere.methods import register_pre_hook, register_post_hook, register_failure_hook

    register_pre_hook("ledger.capture_inputs", _pre_capture_inputs)
    register_post_hook("ledger.append", _post_append)
    register_failure_hook("ledger.append_on_failure", _on_failure_append)

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Any, Optional

from noosphere.models import LedgerEntry
from noosphere.noosphere.ledger.keys import KeyRing

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@dataclass
class EntryIssue:
    entry_id: str
    issue_type: str
    detail: str


@dataclass
class ReplayResult:
    entry_id: str
    method_id: str
    expected_output_hash: str
    actual_output_hash: str
    matched: bool
    skip_reason: str = ""


@dataclass
class VerifyReport:
    total_entries: int = 0
    chain_valid: bool = True
    signatures_valid: bool = True
    issues: list[EntryIssue] = field(default_factory=list)
    replay_results: list[ReplayResult] = field(default_factory=list)

    @property
    def ok(self) -> bool:
        return self.chain_valid and self.signatures_valid and len(self.issues) == 0


def verify(
    store: Any,
    keyring: KeyRing,
    *,
    since: Optional[str] = None,
    until: Optional[str] = None,
    replay: bool = False,
    storage_client: Any = None,
) -> VerifyReport:
    report = VerifyReport()
    prev_hash = ""
    prev_entry_id: Optional[str] = None

    if since is not None:
        before = store.get_ledger_entry(since)
        if before is not None:
            prev_hash = before.prev_hash

    entries: list[LedgerEntry] = list(store.iter_ledger(from_id=since, to_id=until))
    report.total_entries = len(entries)

    for i, entry in enumerate(entries):
        expected_prev = prev_hash if i == 0 and since is None else (
            entries[i - 1].entry_id if i > 0 else prev_hash
        )
        if entry.prev_hash != expected_prev:
            report.chain_valid = False
            report.issues.append(EntryIssue(
                entry_id=entry.entry_id,
                issue_type="chain_break",
                detail=f"prev_hash {entry.prev_hash!r} != expected {expected_prev!r}",
            ))

        sig_ok = keyring.verify(
            entry.entry_id.encode(),
            bytes.fromhex(entry.signature),
            entry.signer_key_id,
        )
        if not sig_ok:
            report.signatures_valid = False
            report.issues.append(EntryIssue(
                entry_id=entry.entry_id,
                issue_type="bad_signature",
                detail=f"Signature verification failed for key_id {entry.signer_key_id!r}",
            ))

        if replay and entry.method_id and storage_client is not None:
            _try_replay(entry, report, storage_client)

    return report


def _try_replay(
    entry: LedgerEntry,
    report: VerifyReport,
    storage_client: Any,
) -> None:
    from noosphere.methods._registry import REGISTRY

    try:
        spec, fn = _find_method_by_id(REGISTRY, entry.method_id)
    except Exception:
        report.replay_results.append(ReplayResult(
            entry_id=entry.entry_id,
            method_id=entry.method_id or "",
            expected_output_hash=entry.outputs_hash,
            actual_output_hash="",
            matched=False,
            skip_reason="method_not_found",
        ))
        return

    if spec.nondeterministic:
        report.replay_results.append(ReplayResult(
            entry_id=entry.entry_id,
            method_id=entry.method_id or "",
            expected_output_hash=entry.outputs_hash,
            actual_output_hash="",
            matched=False,
            skip_reason="nondeterministic",
        ))
        return

    try:
        import json
        raw = storage_client.open_read(key=_ref_to_key(entry.inputs_ref)).read()
        input_data = json.loads(raw)
        result = fn(input_data)
        import json as _json
        from pydantic import BaseModel
        if isinstance(result, BaseModel):
            result = result.model_dump(mode="json")
        actual_hash = hashlib.sha256(
            _json.dumps(result, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str).encode()
        ).hexdigest()
        report.replay_results.append(ReplayResult(
            entry_id=entry.entry_id,
            method_id=entry.method_id or "",
            expected_output_hash=entry.outputs_hash,
            actual_output_hash=actual_hash,
            matched=(actual_hash == entry.outputs_hash),
        ))
        if actual_hash != entry.outputs_hash:
            report.issues.append(EntryIssue(
                entry_id=entry.entry_id,
                issue_type="replay_mismatch",
                detail=f"expected {entry.outputs_hash}, got {actual_hash}",
            ))
    except Exception as e:
        report.replay_results.append(ReplayResult(
            entry_id=entry.entry_id,
            method_id=entry.method_id or "",
            expected_output_hash=entry.outputs_hash,
            actual_output_hash="",
            matched=False,
            skip_reason=f"replay_error: {e}",
        ))


def _find_method_by_id(registry: Any, method_id: str) -> tuple:
    for spec in registry.list():
        if spec.method_id == method_id:
            return registry.get(spec.name, version=spec.version)
    raise LookupError(f"Method {method_id} not found in registry")


def _ref_to_key(ref: str) -> str:
    if ref.startswith("file://"):
        return ref[7:].split("/blobs/", 1)[-1] if "/blobs/" in ref else ref[7:]
    return ref
